content: The human language learning definition of reflexion (as a vocabulary term taught in high school curricula) can act as a grounded alignment anchor for LLM self-reflection mechanisms, similar to how vocabulary acquisition anchors abstract semantic concepts in explicit linguistic context. This means LLM self-reflection agents can be fine-tuned to first internalize the human lexical schema of reflexion itself before performing higher-order self-critique, improving the alignment of AI self-reflection outputs to human expectations of what reflection entails.
